summary: The normal site of implantation is the posterior wall of the fundus of the uterus.
The normal site of implantation is the anterior wall of the cervix.
Implantation normally occurs in the uterine tube.
The trophoblast differentiates into an inner cytotrophoblast and an outer syncytiotrophoblast.
The trophoblast differentiates into an inner syncytiotrophoblast and an outer cytotrophoblast.
The trophoblast remains as a single undifferentiated layer.
The syncytiotrophoblast produces hCG which maintains the corpus luteum and is the basis of pregnancy tests.
The cytotrophoblast produces hCG which causes the corpus luteum to degenerate.
hCG is produced by the embryoblast and acts on the anterior pituitary.
The bilaminar disc consists of the epiblast (dorsal) and hypoblast (ventral).
The bilaminar disc consists of the ectoderm (dorsal) and endoderm (ventral).
The bilaminar disc consists of the cytotrophoblast and syncytiotrophoblast.
The implantation window is Days 20-24 of the menstrual cycle, when the endometrium is receptive.
The implantation window is Days 5-8 of the menstrual cycle.
Implantation can occur at any time during the menstrual cycle.
The amniotic cavity forms above the epiblast and the primary yolk sac forms below the hypoblast.
The amniotic cavity forms below the hypoblast.
The yolk sac forms above the epiblast.
Ectopic implantation most commonly occurs in the ampulla of the uterine tube.
Ectopic implantation most commonly occurs in the isthmus of the uterine tube.
Ectopic implantation most commonly occurs in the ovary.
